title: Melanoma Risk Factors and Early Recognition Context

sections.title: Risk factors
sections.text: Cutaneous melanoma risk is influenced by phenotype, ultraviolet exposure, family history, personal history of melanoma, and the presence of numerous or atypical melanocytic nevi. In a RAG assistant, these factors should be surfaced as clinical context rather than as a way to convert a single image into a diagnosis.

sections.title: Early recognition context
sections.text: Early recognition discussions commonly emphasize evolution of a lesion over time, discordance from surrounding nevi, and prompt specialist review when concern is present. A cautious system should connect these risk factors to evaluation urgency without overstating certainty from limited evidence.
